sub_mechanisms: M_1: treat the pretrained LLM as inducing a prior distribution over solution programs/prompts
M_2: after observing K few-shot examples, form a data-dependent posterior over programs/prompts
M_3: compute KL(posterior ‖ prior) from log-likelihood difference
M_4: derive a non-vacuous test-accuracy bound via McAllester or Catoni form
stripped_llm_application: A bounded few-shot in-context-learning evaluator that treats the pretrained model as a prior over solution programs, forms a posterior from K few-shot examples, computes KL to derive a generalization bound on held-out test accuracy. The bound is non-vacuous when the model already implicitly contains the solution (prior concentrated near posterior).
novelty_claim_stripped: Existing PAC-Bayes for fine-tuning treats weights as the hypothesis. The candidate treats the program/prompt induced by few-shot examples as the hypothesis, leveraging the model itself as the prior.
